terms.definition: Ensemble that adds shallow regression trees sequentially, each fitting pseudo-residuals from the current model. XGBoost and LightGBM are production implementations — boosting loop canonical in Gradient Boosting.
Discretize each numeric feature into bins, aggregate gradient statistics per bin, and evaluate candidate splits only at bin boundaries. Canonical treatment in this topic.
Tree expansion policy that splits the leaf with the largest loss reduction next. LightGBM default; controlled by num_leaves and min_data_in_leaf.
Tree expansion policy that splits all leaves at the same depth before going deeper. XGBoost default style; controlled primarily by max_depth.
Shrinkage factor η applied to each new tree's contribution: F ← F + η·tree. Smaller η usually needs more n_estimators but generalizes better.
Halt boosting when validation metric fails to improve for a set number of rounds (early_stopping_rounds). Prevents overfitting late trees.
Per-split learned branch for rows with NaN on the split feature. XGBoost and LightGBM handle missing in-tree without predict-time imputation.
LightGBM hyperparameter capping total leaves per tree — main complexity knob under leaf-wise growth (not interchangeable with max_depth).
Maximum tree depth — primary depth cap in XGBoost level-wise growth; related but not equivalent to num_leaves in LightGBM.
